site.title: vite-mastery · Master Vite 8, from Rolldown to the Environment API
site.description: An in-depth guide to Vite 8 — Rolldown architecture, the Environment API, hooks and HMR. Finish it and you can ship production-grade plugins.
site.authorBio: Frontend engineer · obsessed with build tools and DX

footer.copyright: © {year} {author}. Content licensed CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, code under MIT.

hero.titleB: from Rolldown to the Environment API.
hero.subtitle: A systematic guide that opens up Rolldown internals, the Environment API and hook timing. Finish it and you can ship production-grade Vite 8 plugins.

whyVite.title: Between "I can use it" and "I get why", this guide closes the gap.
whyVite.lead: Most Vite tutorials stop at the config surface. This guide opens every black box behind it.

questions.q: Why doesn't Vite bundle in dev mode?
When exactly do resolveId / load / transform fire?
What does the WebSocket protocol behind import.meta.hot look like?
How do pre / default / post change plugin behavior?
Why did Vite evolve from esbuild + Rollup to Rolldown?

partsOverview.title: Fourteen parts — from getting started to source-code walk-throughs.
partsOverview.lead: The spine covers bundler evolution, plugins, hooks, the Environment API and HMR. Supporting parts handle framework integration, library mode, SSR, performance and monorepos.

authorIntro.quote: "The guide I wish I had two years ago."
authorIntro.bio: Jay (Yonjay) is a frontend engineer who has spent years on build tools and developer experience. This guide started from one observation: Vite is everywhere, but resources that explain how it actually works internally are rare.

finalCta.title: Ready to go deep on Vite?
finalCta.lead: Starts with "Why we need Vite". Twelve parts, a thousand-plus sections — end to end.

about.metaDescription: About vite-mastery — why this guide exists, who the author is, and how to contribute.
about.title: Why write this guide?
about.intro1: In March 2026, Vite 8 shipped. It brought the unified Rolldown bundler, the Environment API, the @vitejs/devtools early preview and more — yet most tutorials still stop at the Vite 5 era: editing vite.config.ts, tweaking plugin options, running build.
about.intro2: : it opens the core black boxes of Vite 8 and explains them.
promises: Every code sample targets Vite 8.1.x and actually runs
Vite 7 differences live inside <V7Note> blocks — the main text stays on Vite 8
Environment API content is marked as RC, no made-up APIs
Ten companion projects take you from reading to shipping
about.authorIntro: Working on build tools and developer experience for a long time. This guide is part personal learning log for Vite 8, part attempt to help other engineers actually understand how Vite works.
about.contributeIntro: Spotted an error, got a better explanation, want to add a new project? You're welcome to:
about.stackIntro: The site itself is a working sample of the Vite 8 ecosystem:

dep-graph.label: DepGraph
dep-graph.desc: Module Graph visualization — click a node to inspect its import / importer relationships.

v7-note.title: Collapsible behavior diff
v7-note.firstTitle: Vite 7: dev and build used different engines
v7-note.firstIntroMiddle: for transform, while build mode used
v7-note.firstIntroAfter: for bundling. The two engines showed differences in:
firstItems: Some Rollup plugins did not run in dev because esbuild did not execute Rollup hooks.
treeshake only affected build; dev did not tree-shake modules.
CommonJS modules were handled by vite:commonjs in dev and by @rollup/plugin-commonjs in build.
v7-note.firstMigration: Migration to Vite 8: Rolldown unifies the two stages, so these differences disappear. Check whether your plugin relied on esbuild-specific behavior.
v7-note.secondTitle: Vite 7: import.meta.glob did not support eager + as together
v7-note.secondAfter: could throw. You needed to import eagerly first, then read the content manually.
v7-note.secondMigration: Vite 8 fixes this combination, so both options can be used together.
v7-note.defaultBody: This is a V7Note with an explicit English title, matching the component's default usage pattern for short one-line diffs.

bundler-compare.title: Dual-engine architecture comparison
bundler-compare.description: Click or hover nodes to highlight data flow. Left: Vite 7 esbuild + Rollup dual engine; right: Vite 8 Rolldown unified engine.

hook-explorer.title: Hook timing browser
hook-explorer.description: Click a hook name to inspect trigger timing, parameter signature, sample code and Rolldown compatibility.
hook-explorer.filterHint: Use filter to show only specific hooks:

plugin-pipeline.title: Plugin execution order
plugin-pipeline.description: Hover a plugin card to highlight other plugins that share the same hook.

hmr-demo.title: HMR protocol demo
hmr-demo.description: Edit any code in the left editor; the right panel shows simulated Vite HMR WebSocket payloads in real time.

environment-explorer.title: Multi-environment module boundaries
environment-explorer.description: Visualize client / ssr / rsc module graphs and isolation boundaries. Click a module to see where it appears and who imports it.

config-playground.title: Config playground
config-playground.description: Edit vite.config.ts on the left and watch the inferred build output change on the right. Try outDir, assetsDir, sourcemap and manualChunks.

dep-graph.title: Module Graph visualization
dep-graph.description: An interactive dependency graph rendered with @xyflow/react. Drag, zoom and click nodes to inspect import / importer relationships.

00-getting-started.title: Getting started
00-getting-started.summary: What Vite 8 promises, and a working project in five minutes.

01-core-concepts.title: Core concepts
01-core-concepts.summary: Native ESM, the unified Rolldown architecture, dep pre-bundling, the module graph.

02-bundler-evolution.title: Bundler evolution
02-bundler-evolution.summary: From the esbuild + Rollup dual-engine legacy to Rolldown — handoff and compatibility layer.

03-plugin-system.title: Plugin system
03-plugin-system.summary: How Vite / Rolldown / Rollup APIs relate, enforce ordering, and @vitejs/devtools.

04-hooks-deep-dive.title: Hooks deep dive
04-hooks-deep-dive.summary: Universal / Vite-only / Rolldown-extension hooks — the full picture and timing differences.

05-environment-api.title: Environment API
05-environment-api.summary: Multi-environment models beyond client / ssr, framework adapters and RSC-like patterns.

06-hmr.title: HMR
06-hmr.summary: The WebSocket protocol, import.meta.hot, and HotChannel dispatching across environments.

07-build-pipeline.title: Production build
07-build-pipeline.summary: Rolldown chunking, lightningcss, baseline targets, Module Federation.

08-ssr-ssg.title: SSR & SSG
08-ssr-ssg.summary: The new SSR model built on the Environment API, hand-rolled SSR, Wasm SSR.

09-framework-integration.title: Framework integration
09-framework-integration.summary: React v6 (Oxc) / Vue / Svelte / Solid + meta-framework comparison.

10-library-mode.title: Library mode
10-library-mode.summary: build.lib in full, type declarations, a cross-framework component library.

11-monorepo.title: Monorepo engineering
11-monorepo.summary: pnpm workspace, cross-package dep pre-bundling, Turborepo caching strategy.

13-real-world-plugins.title: Real-world plugins
13-real-world-plugins.summary: Source-code reading: auto-import / plugin-pages / plugin-react v6.
